<html lang="en" class="js-focus-visible js" data-platform="Linux x86_64" data-readthedocs-tool="zensical" data-js-focus-visible=""><head>
    
      <meta charset="utf-8">
      <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width,initial-scale=1">
      
        <meta name="description" content="Scrapling - Effortless Web Scraping for the Modern Web!">
      
      
        <meta name="author" content="Karim Shoair">
      
      
        <style type="text/css">:root, :host {
  --fa-font-solid: normal 900 1em/1 'Font Awesome 7 Free';
  --fa-font-regular: normal 400 1em/1 'Font Awesome 7 Free';
  --fa-font-light: normal 300 1em/1 'Font Awesome 7 Pro';
  --fa-font-thin: normal 100 1em/1 'Font Awesome 7 Pro';
  --fa-font-duotone: normal 900 1em/1 'Font Awesome 7 Duotone';
  --fa-font-duotone-regular: normal 400 1em/1 'Font Awesome 7 Duotone';
  --fa-font-duotone-light: normal 300 1em/1 'Font Awesome 7 Duotone';
  --fa-font-duotone-thin: normal 100 1em/1 'Font Awesome 7 Duotone';
  --fa-font-brands: normal 400 1em/1 'Font Awesome 7 Brands';
  --fa-font-sharp-solid: normal 900 1em/1 'Font Awesome 7 Sharp';
  --fa-font-sharp-regular: normal 400 1em/1 'Font Awesome 7 Sharp';
  --fa-font-sharp-light: normal 300 1em/1 'Font Awesome 7 Sharp';
  --fa-font-sharp-thin: normal 100 1em/1 'Font Awesome 7 Sharp';
  --fa-font-sharp-duotone-solid: normal 900 1em/1 'Font Awesome 7 Sharp Duotone';
  --fa-font-sharp-duotone-regular: normal 400 1em/1 'Font Awesome 7 Sharp Duotone';
  --fa-font-sharp-duotone-light: normal 300 1em/1 'Font Awesome 7 Sharp Duotone';
  --fa-font-sharp-duotone-thin: normal 100 1em/1 'Font Awesome 7 Sharp Duotone';
  --fa-font-slab-regular: normal 400 1em/1 'Font Awesome 7 Slab';
  --fa-font-slab-press-regular: normal 400 1em/1 'Font Awesome 7 Slab Press';
  --fa-font-whiteboard-semibold: normal 600 1em/1 'Font Awesome 7 Whiteboard';
  --fa-font-thumbprint-light: normal 300 1em/1 'Font Awesome 7 Thumbprint';
  --fa-font-notdog-solid: normal 900 1em/1 'Font Awesome 7 Notdog';
  --fa-font-notdog-duo-solid: normal 900 1em/1 'Font Awesome 7 Notdog Duo';
  --fa-font-etch-solid: normal 900 1em/1 'Font Awesome 7 Etch';
  --fa-font-graphite-thin: normal 100 1em/1 'Font Awesome 7 Graphite';
  --fa-font-jelly-regular: normal 400 1em/1 'Font Awesome 7 Jelly';
  --fa-font-jelly-fill-regular: normal 400 1em/1 'Font Awesome 7 Jelly Fill';
  --fa-font-jelly-duo-regular: normal 400 1em/1 'Font Awesome 7 Jelly Duo';
  --fa-font-chisel-regular: normal 400 1em/1 'Font Awesome 7 Chisel';
  --fa-font-utility-semibold: normal 600 1em/1 'Font Awesome 7 Utility';
  --fa-font-utility-duo-semibold: normal 600 1em/1 'Font Awesome 7 Utility Duo';
  --fa-font-utility-fill-semibold: normal 600 1em/1 'Font Awesome 7 Utility Fill';
}

.svg-inline--fa {
  box-sizing: content-box;
  display: var(--fa-display, inline-block);
  height: 1em;
  overflow: visible;
  vertical-align: -0.125em;
  width: var(--fa-width, 1.25em);
}
.svg-inline--fa.fa-2xs {
  vertical-align: 0.1em;
}
.svg-inline--fa.fa-xs {
  vertical-align: 0em;
}
.svg-inline--fa.fa-sm {
  vertical-align: -0.0714285714em;
}
.svg-inline--fa.fa-lg {
  vertical-align: -0.2em;
}
.svg-inline--fa.fa-xl {
  vertical-align: -0.25em;
}
.svg-inline--fa.fa-2xl {
  vertical-align: -0.3125em;
}
.svg-inline--fa.fa-pull-left,
.svg-inline--fa .fa-pull-start {
  float: inline-start;
  margin-inline-end: var(--fa-pull-margin, 0.3em);
}
.svg-inline--fa.fa-pull-right,
.svg-inline--fa .fa-pull-end {
  float: inline-end;
  margin-inline-start: var(--fa-pull-margin, 0.3em);
}
.svg-inline--fa.fa-li {
  width: var(--fa-li-width, 2em);
  inset-inline-start: calc(-1 * var(--fa-li-width, 2em));
  inset-block-start: 0.25em; /* syncing vertical alignment with Web Font rendering */
}

.fa-layers-counter, .fa-layers-text {
  display: inline-block;
  position: absolute;
  text-align: center;
}

.fa-layers {
  display: inline-block;
  height: 1em;
  position: relative;
  text-align: center;
  vertical-align: -0.125em;
  width: var(--fa-width, 1.25em);
}
.fa-layers .svg-inline--fa {
  inset: 0;
  margin: auto;
  position: absolute;
  transform-origin: center center;
}

.fa-layers-text {
  left: 50%;
  top: 50%;
  transform: translate(-50%, -50%);
  transform-origin: center center;
}

.fa-layers-counter {
  background-color: var(--fa-counter-background-color, #ff253a);
  border-radius: var(--fa-counter-border-radius, 1em);
  box-sizing: border-box;
  color: var(--fa-inverse, #fff);
  line-height: var(--fa-counter-line-height, 1);
  max-width: var(--fa-counter-max-width, 5em);
  min-width: var(--fa-counter-min-width, 1.5em);
  overflow: hidden;
  padding: var(--fa-counter-padding, 0.25em 0.5em);
  right: var(--fa-right, 0);
  text-overflow: ellipsis;
  top: var(--fa-top, 0);
  transform: scale(var(--fa-counter-scale, 0.25));
  transform-origin: top right;
}

.fa-layers-bottom-right {
  bottom: var(--fa-bottom, 0);
  right: var(--fa-right, 0);
  top: auto;
  transform: scale(var(--fa-layers-scale, 0.25));
  transform-origin: bottom right;
}

.fa-layers-bottom-left {
  bottom: var(--fa-bottom, 0);
  left: var(--fa-left, 0);
  right: auto;
  top: auto;
  transform: scale(var(--fa-layers-scale, 0.25));
  transform-origin: bottom left;
}

.fa-layers-top-right {
  top: var(--fa-top, 0);
  right: var(--fa-right, 0);
  transform: scale(var(--fa-layers-scale, 0.25));
  transform-origin: top right;
}

.fa-layers-top-left {
  left: var(--fa-left, 0);
  right: auto;
  top: var(--fa-top, 0);
  transform: scale(var(--fa-layers-scale, 0.25));
  transform-origin: top left;
}

.fa-1x {
  font-size: 1em;
}

.fa-2x {
  font-size: 2em;
}

.fa-3x {
  font-size: 3em;
}

.fa-4x {
  font-size: 4em;
}

.fa-5x {
  font-size: 5em;
}

.fa-6x {
  font-size: 6em;
}

.fa-7x {
  font-size: 7em;
}

.fa-8x {
  font-size: 8em;
}

.fa-9x {
  font-size: 9em;
}

.fa-10x {
  font-size: 10em;
}

.fa-2xs {
  font-size: calc(10 / 16 * 1em); /* converts a 10px size into an em-based value that's relative to the scale's 16px base */
  line-height: calc(1 / 10 * 1em); /* sets the line-height of the icon back to that of it's parent */
  vertical-align: calc((6 / 10 - 0.375) * 1em); /* vertically centers the icon taking into account the surrounding text's descender */
}

.fa-xs {
  font-size: calc(12 / 16 * 1em); /* converts a 12px size into an em-based value that's relative to the scale's 16px base */
  line-height: calc(1 / 12 * 1em); /* sets the line-height of the icon back to that of it's parent */
  vertical-align: calc((6 / 12 - 0.375) * 1em); /* vertically centers the icon taking into account the surrounding text's descender */
}

.fa-sm {
  font-size: calc(14 / 16 * 1em); /* converts a 14px size into an em-based value that's relative to the scale's 16px base */
  line-height: calc(1 / 14 * 1em); /* sets the line-height of the icon back to that of it's parent */
  vertical-align: calc((6 / 14 - 0.375) * 1em); /* vertically centers the icon taking into account the surrounding text's descender */
}

.fa-lg {
  font-size: calc(20 / 16 * 1em); /* converts a 20px size into an em-based value that's relative to the scale's 16px base */
  line-height: calc(1 / 20 * 1em); /* sets the line-height of the icon back to that of it's parent */
  vertical-align: calc((6 / 20 - 0.375) * 1em); /* vertically centers the icon taking into account the surrounding text's descender */
}

.fa-xl {
  font-size: calc(24 / 16 * 1em); /* converts a 24px size into an em-based value that's relative to the scale's 16px base */
  line-height: calc(1 / 24 * 1em); /* sets the line-height of the icon back to that of it's parent */
  vertical-align: calc((6 / 24 - 0.375) * 1em); /* vertically centers the icon taking into account the surrounding text's descender */
}

.fa-2xl {
  font-size: calc(32 / 16 * 1em); /* converts a 32px size into an em-based value that's relative to the scale's 16px base */
  line-height: calc(1 / 32 * 1em); /* sets the line-height of the icon back to that of it's parent */
  vertical-align: calc((6 / 32 - 0.375) * 1em); /* vertically centers the icon taking into account the surrounding text's descender */
}

.fa-width-auto {
  --fa-width: auto;
}

.fa-fw,
.fa-width-fixed {
  --fa-width: 1.25em;
}

.fa-ul {
  list-style-type: none;
  margin-inline-start: var(--fa-li-margin, 2.5em);
  padding-inline-start: 0;
}
.fa-ul > li {
  position: relative;
}

.fa-li {
  inset-inline-start: calc(-1 * var(--fa-li-width, 2em));
  position: absolute;
  text-align: center;
  width: var(--fa-li-width, 2em);
  line-height: inherit;
}

/* Heads Up: Bordered Icons will not be supported in the future!
  - This feature will be deprecated in the next major release of Font Awesome (v8)!
  - You may continue to use it in this version *v7), but it will not be supported in Font Awesome v8.
*/
/* Notes:
* --@{v.$css-prefix}-border-width = 1/16 by default (to render as ~1px based on a 16px default font-size)
* --@{v.$css-prefix}-border-padding =
  ** 3/16 for vertical padding (to give ~2px of vertical whitespace around an icon considering it's vertical alignment)
  ** 4/16 for horizontal padding (to give ~4px of horizontal whitespace around an icon)
*/
.fa-border {
  border-color: var(--fa-border-color, #eee);
  border-radius: var(--fa-border-radius, 0.1em);
  border-style: var(--fa-border-style, solid);
  border-width: var(--fa-border-width, 0.0625em);
  box-sizing: var(--fa-border-box-sizing, content-box);
  padding: var(--fa-border-padding, 0.1875em 0.25em);
}

.fa-pull-left,
.fa-pull-start {
  float: inline-start;
  margin-inline-end: var(--fa-pull-margin, 0.3em);
}

.fa-pull-right,
.fa-pull-end {
  float: inline-end;
  margin-inline-start: var(--fa-pull-margin, 0.3em);
}

.fa-beat {
  animation-name: fa-beat;
  animation-delay: var(--fa-animation-delay, 0s);
  animation-direction: var(--fa-animation-direction, normal);
  animation-duration: var(--fa-animation-duration, 1s);
  animation-iteration-count: var(--fa-animation-iteration-count, infinite);
  animation-timing-function: var(--fa-animation-timing, ease-in-out);
}

.fa-bounce {
  animation-name: fa-bounce;
  animation-delay: var(--fa-animation-delay, 0s);
  animation-direction: var(--fa-animation-direction, normal);
  animation-duration: var(--fa-animation-duration, 1s);
  animation-iteration-count: var(--fa-animation-iteration-count, infinite);
  animation-timing-function: var(--fa-animation-timing, cubic-bezier(0.28, 0.84, 0.42, 1));
}

.fa-fade {
  animation-name: fa-fade;
  animation-delay: var(--fa-animation-delay, 0s);
  animation-direction: var(--fa-animation-direction, normal);
  animation-duration: var(--fa-animation-duration, 1s);
  animation-iteration-count: var(--fa-animation-iteration-count, infinite);
  animation-timing-function: var(--fa-animation-timing, cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.6, 1));
}

.fa-beat-fade {
  animation-name: fa-beat-fade;
  animation-delay: var(--fa-animation-delay, 0s);
  animation-direction: var(--fa-animation-direction, normal);
  animation-duration: var(--fa-animation-duration, 1s);
  animation-iteration-count: var(--fa-animation-iteration-count, infinite);
  animation-timing-function: var(--fa-animation-timing, cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.6, 1));
}

.fa-flip {
  animation-name: fa-flip;
  animation-delay: var(--fa-animation-delay, 0s);
  animation-direction: var(--fa-animation-direction, normal);
  animation-duration: var(--fa-animation-duration, 1s);
  animation-iteration-count: var(--fa-animation-iteration-count, infinite);
  animation-timing-function: var(--fa-animation-timing, ease-in-out);
}

.fa-shake {
  animation-name: fa-shake;
  animation-delay: var(--fa-animation-delay, 0s);
  animation-direction: var(--fa-animation-direction, normal);
  animation-duration: var(--fa-animation-duration, 1s);
  animation-iteration-count: var(--fa-animation-iteration-count, infinite);
  animation-timing-function: var(--fa-animation-timing, linear);
}

.fa-spin {
  animation-name: fa-spin;
  animation-delay: var(--fa-animation-delay, 0s);
  animation-direction: var(--fa-animation-direction, normal);
  animation-duration: var(--fa-animation-duration, 2s);
  animation-iteration-count: var(--fa-animation-iteration-count, infinite);
  animation-timing-function: var(--fa-animation-timing, linear);
}

.fa-spin-reverse {
  --fa-animation-direction: reverse;
}

.fa-pulse,
.fa-spin-pulse {
  animation-name: fa-spin;
  animation-direction: var(--fa-animation-direction, normal);
  animation-duration: var(--fa-animation-duration, 1s);
  animation-iteration-count: var(--fa-animation-iteration-count, infinite);
  animation-timing-function: var(--fa-animation-timing, steps(8));
}

@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) {
  .fa-beat,
  .fa-bounce,
  .fa-fade,
  .fa-beat-fade,
  .fa-flip,
  .fa-pulse,
  .fa-shake,
  .fa-spin,
  .fa-spin-pulse {
    animation: none !important;
    transition: none !important;
  }
}
@keyframes fa-beat {
  0%, 90% {
    transform: scale(1);
  }
  45% {
    transform: scale(var(--fa-beat-scale, 1.25));
  }
}
@keyframes fa-bounce {
  0% {
    transform: scale(1, 1) translateY(0);
  }
  10% {
    transform: scale(var(--fa-bounce-start-scale-x, 1.1), var(--fa-bounce-start-scale-y, 0.9)) translateY(0);
  }
  30% {
    transform: scale(var(--fa-bounce-jump-scale-x, 0.9), var(--fa-bounce-jump-scale-y, 1.1)) translateY(var(--fa-bounce-height, -0.5em));
  }
  50% {
    transform: scale(var(--fa-bounce-land-scale-x, 1.05), var(--fa-bounce-land-scale-y, 0.95)) translateY(0);
  }
  57% {
    transform: scale(1, 1) translateY(var(--fa-bounce-rebound, -0.125em));
  }
  64% {
    transform: scale(1, 1) translateY(0);
  }
  100% {
    transform: scale(1, 1) translateY(0);
  }
}
@keyframes fa-fade {
  50% {
    opacity: var(--fa-fade-opacity, 0.4);
  }
}
@keyframes fa-beat-fade {
  0%, 100% {
    opacity: var(--fa-beat-fade-opacity, 0.4);
    transform: scale(1);
  }
  50% {
    opacity: 1;
    transform: scale(var(--fa-beat-fade-scale, 1.125));
  }
}
@keyframes fa-flip {
  50% {
    transform: rotate3d(var(--fa-flip-x, 0), var(--fa-flip-y, 1), var(--fa-flip-z, 0), var(--fa-flip-angle, -180deg));
  }
}
@keyframes fa-shake {
  0% {
    transform: rotate(-15deg);
  }
  4% {
    transform: rotate(15deg);
  }
  8%, 24% {
    transform: rotate(-18deg);
  }
  12%, 28% {
    transform: rotate(18deg);
  }
  16% {
    transform: rotate(-22deg);
  }
  20% {
    transform: rotate(22deg);
  }
  32% {
    transform: rotate(-12deg);
  }
  36% {
    transform: rotate(12deg);
  }
  40%, 100% {
    transform: rotate(0deg);
  }
}
@keyframes fa-spin {
  0% {
    transform: rotate(0deg);
  }
  100% {
    transform: rotate(360deg);
  }
}
.fa-rotate-90 {
  transform: rotate(90deg);
}

.fa-rotate-180 {
  transform: rotate(180deg);
}

.fa-rotate-270 {
  transform: rotate(270deg);
}

.fa-flip-horizontal {
  transform: scale(-1, 1);
}

.fa-flip-vertical {
  transform: scale(1, -1);
}

.fa-flip-both,
.fa-flip-horizontal.fa-flip-vertical {
  transform: scale(-1, -1);
}

.fa-rotate-by {
  transform: rotate(var(--fa-rotate-angle, 0));
}

.svg-inline--fa .fa-primary {
  fill: var(--fa-primary-color, currentColor);
  opacity: var(--fa-primary-opacity, 1);
}

.svg-inline--fa .fa-secondary {
  fill: var(--fa-secondary-color, currentColor);
  opacity: var(--fa-secondary-opacity, 0.4);
}

.svg-inline--fa.fa-swap-opacity .fa-primary {
  opacity: var(--fa-secondary-opacity, 0.4);
}

.svg-inline--fa.fa-swap-opacity .fa-secondary {
  opacity: var(--fa-primary-opacity, 1);
}

.svg-inline--fa mask .fa-primary,
.svg-inline--fa mask .fa-secondary {
  fill: black;
}

.svg-inline--fa.fa-inverse {
  fill: var(--fa-inverse, #fff);
}

.fa-stack {
  display: inline-block;
  height: 2em;
  line-height: 2em;
  position: relative;
  vertical-align: middle;
  width: 2.5em;
}

.fa-inverse {
  color: var(--fa-inverse, #fff);
}

.svg-inline--fa.fa-stack-1x {
  --fa-width: 1.25em;
  height: 1em;
  width: var(--fa-width);
}
.svg-inline--fa.fa-stack-2x {
  --fa-width: 2.5em;
  height: 2em;
  width: var(--fa-width);
}

.fa-stack-1x,
.fa-stack-2x {
  inset: 0;
  margin: auto;
  position: absolute;
  z-index: var(--fa-stack-z-index, auto);
}</style><link rel="canonical" href="https://scrapling.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api-reference/fetchers.html">

<h3 id="scrapling.fetchers.DynamicFetcher.fetch" class="doc doc-heading">
<code class="doc-symbol doc-symbol-heading doc-symbol-method"></code>            <span class="doc doc-object-name doc-function-name">fetch</span>


  <span class="doc doc-labels">
      <small class="doc doc-label doc-label-classmethod"><code>classmethod</code></small>
  </span>

<a href="https://scrapling.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api-reference/fetchers.html#scrapling.fetchers.DynamicFetcher.fetch" class="headerlink" title="Permanent link">¶</a></h3>
<div class="language-python doc-signature highlight"><pre id="__code_62"><span></span><nav class="md-code__nav"><button class="md-code__button" title="Copy to clipboard" data-clipboard-target="#__code_62 > code" data-md-type="copy"></button></nav><code class="md-code__content"><span id="__span-0-1"><a id="__codelineno-0-1" name="__codelineno-0-1" href="https://scrapling.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api-reference/fetchers.html#__codelineno-0-1"></a><span class="nf">fetch</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">url</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="o">**</span><span class="n">kwargs</span><span class="p">)</span>
</span></code></pre></div>

    <div class="doc doc-contents ">

        <p>Opens up a browser and do your request based on your chosen options below.</p>


<div class="md-typeset__scrollwrap"><div class="md-typeset__table"><table>
      <thead>
        <tr>
          <th><span class="doc-section-title">PARAMETER</span></th>
          <th><span>DESCRIPTION</span></th>
        </tr>
      </thead>
      <tbody>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>url</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>Target url.</p>
              </div>
              <p>
                  <span class="doc-param-annotation">
                    <b>TYPE:</b>
                      <code><a class="autorefs autorefs-external" href="https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str">str</a></code>
                  </span>
              </p>
            </td>
          </tr>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>headless</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>Run the browser in headless/hidden (default), or headful/visible mode.</p>
              </div>
              <p>
              </p>
            </td>
          </tr>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>disable_resources</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>Drop requests for unnecessary resources for a speed boost.</p>
              </div>
              <p>
              </p>
            </td>
          </tr>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>blocked_domains</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>A set of domain names to block requests to. Subdomains are also matched (e.g., <code>"example.com"</code> blocks <code>"sub.example.com"</code> too).</p>
              </div>
              <p>
              </p>
            </td>
          </tr>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>block_ads</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>Block requests to ~3,500 known ad/tracking domains. Can be combined with <code>blocked_domains</code>.</p>
              </div>
              <p>
              </p>
            </td>
          </tr>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>dns_over_https</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>Route DNS queries through Cloudflare's DNS-over-HTTPS to prevent DNS leaks when using proxies.</p>
              </div>
              <p>
              </p>
            </td>
          </tr>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>useragent</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>Pass a useragent string to be used. Otherwise the fetcher will generate a real Useragent of the same browser and use it.</p>
              </div>
              <p>
              </p>
            </td>
          </tr>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>cookies</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>Set cookies for the next request.</p>
              </div>
              <p>
              </p>
            </td>
          </tr>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>network_idle</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">

<h3 id="scrapling.fetchers.DynamicFetcher.async_fetch" class="doc doc-heading">
<code class="doc-symbol doc-symbol-heading doc-symbol-method"></code>            <span class="doc doc-object-name doc-function-name">async_fetch</span>


  <span class="doc doc-labels">
      <small class="doc doc-label doc-label-async"><code>async</code></small>
      <small class="doc doc-label doc-label-classmethod"><code>classmethod</code></small>
  </span>

<a href="https://scrapling.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api-reference/fetchers.html#scrapling.fetchers.DynamicFetcher.async_fetch" class="headerlink" title="Permanent link">¶</a></h3>
<div class="language-python doc-signature highlight"><pre id="__code_64"><span></span><nav class="md-code__nav"><button class="md-code__button" title="Copy to clipboard" data-clipboard-target="#__code_64 > code" data-md-type="copy"></button></nav><code class="md-code__content"><span id="__span-0-1"><a id="__codelineno-0-1" name="__codelineno-0-1" href="https://scrapling.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api-reference/fetchers.html#__codelineno-0-1"></a><span class="nf">async_fetch</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">url</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="o">**</span><span class="n">kwargs</span><span class="p">)</span>
</span></code></pre></div>

    <div class="doc doc-contents ">

        <p>Opens up a browser and do your request based on your chosen options below.</p>


<div class="md-typeset__scrollwrap"><div class="md-typeset__table"><table>
      <thead>
        <tr>
          <th><span class="doc-section-title">PARAMETER</span></th>
          <th><span>DESCRIPTION</span></th>
        </tr>
      </thead>
      <tbody>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>url</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>Target url.</p>
              </div>
              <p>
                  <span class="doc-param-annotation">
                    <b>TYPE:</b>
                      <code><a class="autorefs autorefs-external" href="https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str">str</a></code>
                  </span>
              </p>
            </td>
          </tr>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>headless</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>Run the browser in headless/hidden (default), or headful/visible mode.</p>
              </div>
              <p>
              </p>
            </td>
          </tr>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>disable_resources</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>Drop requests for unnecessary resources for a speed boost.</p>
              </div>
              <p>
              </p>
            </td>
          </tr>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>blocked_domains</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>A set of domain names to block requests to. Subdomains are also matched (e.g., <code>"example.com"</code> blocks <code>"sub.example.com"</code> too).</p>
              </div>
              <p>
              </p>
            </td>
          </tr>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>block_ads</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>Block requests to ~3,500 known ad/tracking domains. Can be combined with <code>blocked_domains</code>.</p>
              </div>
              <p>
              </p>
            </td>
          </tr>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>dns_over_https</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>Route DNS queries through Cloudflare's DNS-over-HTTPS to prevent DNS leaks when using proxies.</p>
              </div>
              <p>
              </p>
            </td>
          </tr>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>useragent</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>Pass a useragent string to be used. Otherwise the fetcher will generate a real Useragent of the same browser and use it.</p>
              </div>
              <p>
              </p>
            </td>
          </tr>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>cookies</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>Set cookies for the next request.</p>
              </div>
              <p>
              </p>
            </td>
          </tr>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>network_idle</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>Wait for the page until there are no network connections for at least 500 ms.</p>
              </div>
              <p>
              </p>
            </td>
          </tr>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>load_dom</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>Enabled by default, wait for all JavaScript on page(s) to fully load and execute.</p>
              </div>
              <p>
              </p>
            </td>
          </tr>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>timeout</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>The timeout in milliseconds that is used in all operations and waits through the page. The default is 30,000</p>
              </div>
              <p>
              </p>
            </td>
          </tr>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>wait</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>The time (milliseconds) the fetcher will wait after everything finishes before closing the page and returning the Response object.</p>
              </div>
              <p>
              </p>
            </td>
          </tr>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>page_action</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>Added for automation. A function that takes the <code>page</code> object, runs after navigation, and does the automation you need.</p>
              </div>
              <p>
              </p>
            </td>
          </tr>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>page_setup</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>A function that takes the <code>page</code> object, runs before navigation. Use it to register event listeners or routes that must be set up before the page loads.</p>
              </div>
              <p>
              </p>
            </td>
          </tr>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>wait_selector</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>Wait for a specific CSS selector to be in a specific state.</p>
              </div>
              <p>
              </p>
            </td>
          </tr>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>init_script</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>An absolute path to a JavaScript file to be executed on page creation with this request.</p>
              </div>
              <p>
              </p>
            </td>
          </tr>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>locale</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>Set the locale for the browser if wanted. Defaults to the system default locale.</p>
              </div>
              <p>
              </p>
            </td>
          </tr>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>wait_selector_state</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>The state to wait for the selector given with <code>wait_selector</code>. The default state is <code>attached</code>.</p>
              </div>
              <p>
              </p>
            </td>
          </tr>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>real_chrome</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>If you have a Chrome browser installed on your device, enable this, and the Fetcher will launch an instance of your browser and use it.</p>
              </div>
              <p>
              </p>
            </td>
          </tr>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>cdp_url</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>Instead of launching a new browser instance, connect to this CDP URL to control real browsers through CDP.</p>
              </div>
              <p>
              </p>
            </td>
          </tr>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>google_search</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>Enabled by default, Scrapling will set a Google referer header.</p>
              </div>
              <p>
              </p>
            </td>
          </tr>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>extra_headers</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>A dictionary of extra headers to add to the request.</p>
              </div>
              <p>
              </p>
            </td>
          </tr>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>proxy</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>The proxy to be used with requests, it can be a string or a dictionary with the keys 'server', 'username', and 'password' only.</p>
              </div>
              <p>
              </p>
            </td>
          </tr>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>extra_flags</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>A list of additional browser flags to pass to the browser on launch.</p>
              </div>
              <p>
              </p>
            </td>
          </tr>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>selector_config</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>The arguments that will be passed in the end while creating the final Selector's class.</p>
              </div>
              <p>
              </p>
            </td>
          </tr>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>additional_args</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>Additional arguments to be passed to Playwright's context as additional settings.</p>
              </div>
              <p>
              </p>
            </td>
          </tr>
      </tbody>
    </table></div></div>

        <p>A <code>Fetcher</code> class type which is a completely stealthy built on top of Chromium.</p>
<p>It works as real browsers passing almost all online tests/protections with many customization options.</p>

<h3 id="scrapling.fetchers.StealthyFetcher.fetch" class="doc doc-heading">
<code class="doc-symbol doc-symbol-heading doc-symbol-method"></code>            <span class="doc doc-object-name doc-function-name">fetch</span>


  <span class="doc doc-labels">
      <small class="doc doc-label doc-label-classmethod"><code>classmethod</code></small>
  </span>

<a href="https://scrapling.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api-reference/fetchers.html#scrapling.fetchers.StealthyFetcher.fetch" class="headerlink" title="Permanent link">¶</a></h3>
<div class="language-python doc-signature highlight"><pre id="__code_81"><span></span><nav class="md-code__nav"><button class="md-code__button" title="Copy to clipboard" data-clipboard-target="#__code_81 > code" data-md-type="copy"></button></nav><code class="md-code__content"><span id="__span-0-1"><a id="__codelineno-0-1" name="__codelineno-0-1" href="https://scrapling.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api-reference/fetchers.html#__codelineno-0-1"></a><span class="nf">fetch</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">url</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="o">**</span><span class="n">kwargs</span><span class="p">)</span>
</span></code></pre></div>

    <div class="doc doc-contents ">

        <p>Opens up a browser and do your request based on your chosen options below.</p>


<div class="md-typeset__scrollwrap"><div class="md-typeset__table"><table>
      <thead>
        <tr>
          <th><span class="doc-section-title">PARAMETER</span></th>
          <th><span>DESCRIPTION</span></th>
        </tr>
      </thead>
      <tbody>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>url</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>Target url.</p>
              </div>
              <p>
                  <span class="doc-param-annotation">
                    <b>TYPE:</b>
                      <code><a class="autorefs autorefs-external" href="https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str">str</a></code>
                  </span>
              </p>
            </td>
          </tr>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>headless</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>Run the browser in headless/hidden (default), or headful/visible mode.</p>
              </div>
              <p>
              </p>
            </td>
          </tr>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>disable_resources</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>Drop requests for unnecessary resources for a speed boost.
Requests dropped are of type <code>font</code>, <code>image</code>, <code>media</code>, <code>beacon</code>, <code>object</code>, <code>imageset</code>, <code>texttrack</code>, <code>websocket</code>, <code>csp_report</code>, and <code>stylesheet</code>.</p>
              </div>
              <p>
              </p>
            </td>
          </tr>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>blocked_domains</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>A set of domain names to block requests to. Subdomains are also matched (e.g., <code>"example.com"</code> blocks <code>"sub.example.com"</code> too).</p>
              </div>
              <p>
              </p>
            </td>
          </tr>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>block_ads</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>Block requests to ~3,500 known ad/tracking domains. Can be combined with <code>blocked_domains</code>.</p>
              </div>
              <p>
              </p>
            </td>
          </tr>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>dns_over_https</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>Route DNS queries through Cloudflare's DNS-over-HTTPS to prevent DNS leaks when using proxies.</p>
              </div>
              <p>
              </p>
            </td>
          </tr>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>useragent</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>Pass a useragent string to be used. Otherwise the fetcher will generate a real Useragent of the same browser and use it.</p>
              </div>
              <p>
              </p>
            </td>
          </tr>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>cookies</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>Set cookies for the next request.</p>
              </div>
              <p>
              </p>
            </td>
          </tr>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>network_idle</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>Wait for the page until there are no network connections for at least 500 ms.</p>
              </div>
              <p>
              </p>
            </td>
          </tr>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>timeout</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>The timeout in milliseconds that is used in all operations and waits through the page. The default is 30,000</p>
              </div>
              <p>
              </p>
            </td>
          </tr>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>wait</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>The time (milliseconds) the fetcher will wait after everything finishes before closing the page and returning the <code>Response</code> object.</p>
              </div>
              <p>
              </p>
            </td>
          </tr>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>page_action</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>Added for automation. A function that takes the <code>page</code> object, runs after navigation, and does the automation you need.</p>
              </div>
              <p>
              </p>
            </td>
          </tr>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>page_setup</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>A function that takes the <code>page</code> object, runs before navigation. Use it to register event listeners or routes that must be set up before the page loads.</p>
              </div>
              <p>
              </p>
            </td>
          </tr>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>wait_selector</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>Wait for a specific CSS selector to be in a specific state.</p>
              </div>
              <p>
              </p>
            </td>
          </tr>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>init_script</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>An absolute path to a JavaScript file to be executed on page creation for all pages in this session.</p>
              </div>
              <p>
              </p>
            </td>
          </tr>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>locale</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>Specify user locale, for example, <code>en-GB</code>, <code>de-DE</code>, etc. Locale will affect navigator.language value, Accept-Language request header value as well as number and date formatting
rules. Defaults to the system default locale.</p>
              </div>
              <p>
              </p>
            </td>
          </tr>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>timezone_id</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>Changes the timezone of the browser. Defaults to the system timezone.</p>
              </div>
              <p>
              </p>
            </td>
          </tr>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>wait_selector_state</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>The state to wait for the selector given with <code>wait_selector</code>. The default state is <code>attached</code>.</p>
              </div>
              <p>
              </p>
            </td>
          </tr>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>solve_cloudflare</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>Solves all types of the Cloudflare's Turnstile/Interstitial challenges before returning the response to you.</p>
              </div>
              <p>
              </p>
            </td>
          </tr>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>real_chrome</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>If you have a Chrome browser installed on your device, enable this, and the Fetcher will launch an instance of your browser and use it.</p>
              </div>
              <p>
              </p>
            </td>
          </tr>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>hide_canvas</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>Add random noise to canvas operations to prevent fingerprinting.</p>
              </div>
              <p>
              </p>
            </td>
          </tr>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>block_webrtc</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>Forces WebRTC to respect proxy settings to prevent local IP address leak.</p>
              </div>
              <p>
              </p>
            </td>
          </tr>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>allow_webgl</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>Enabled by default. Disabling it disables WebGL and WebGL 2.0 support entirely. Disabling WebGL is not recommended as many WAFs now check if WebGL is enabled.</p>
              </div>
              <p>
              </p>
            </td>
          </tr>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>load_dom</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>Enabled by default, wait for all JavaScript on page(s) to fully load and execute.</p>
              </div>
              <p>
              </p>
            </td>
          </tr>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>cdp_url</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>Instead of launching a new browser instance, connect to this CDP URL to control real browsers through CDP.</p>
              </div>
              <p>
              </p>
            </td>
          </tr>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>google_search</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>Enabled by default, Scrapling will set a Google referer header.</p>
              </div>
              <p>
              </p>
            </td>
          </tr>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>extra_headers</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>A dictionary of extra headers to add to the request. <em>The referer set by <code>google_search</code> takes priority over the referer set here if used together.</em></p>
              </div>
              <p>
              </p>
            </td>
          </tr>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>proxy</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>The proxy to be used with requests, it can be a string or a dictionary with the keys 'server', 'username', and 'password' only.</p>
              </div>
              <p>
              </p>
            </td>
          </tr>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>user_data_dir</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>Path to a User Data Directory, which stores browser session data like cookies and local storage. The default is to create a temporary directory.</p>
              </div>
              <p>
              </p>
            </td>
          </tr>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>extra_flags</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>A list of additional browser flags to pass to the browser on launch.</p>
              </div>
              <p>
              </p>
            </td>
          </tr>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>selector_config</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>The arguments that will be passed in the end while creating the final Selector's class.</p>
              </div>
              <p>
              </p>
            </td>
          </tr>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>additional_args</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>Additional arguments to be passed to Playwright's context as additional settings, and it takes higher priority than Scrapling's settings.</p>
              </div>
              <p>
              </p>
            </td>
          </tr>
      </tbody>
    </table></div></div>

</span><span id="__span-0-16"><a id="__codelineno-0-16" name="__codelineno-0-16"></a><span class="sd">    Opens up a browser and do your request based on your chosen options below.</span>

</span><span id="__span-0-18"><a id="__codelineno-0-18" name="__codelineno-0-18"></a><span class="sd">    :param url: Target url.</span>
</span><span id="__span-0-19"><a id="__codelineno-0-19" name="__codelineno-0-19"></a><span class="sd">    :param headless: Run the browser in headless/hidden (default), or headful/visible mode.</span>
</span><span id="__span-0-20"><a id="__codelineno-0-20" name="__codelineno-0-20"></a><span class="sd">    :param disable_resources: Drop requests for unnecessary resources for a speed boost.</span>

</span><span id="__span-0-22"><a id="__codelineno-0-22" name="__codelineno-0-22"></a><span class="sd">    :param blocked_domains: A set of domain names to block requests to. Subdomains are also matched (e.g., ``"example.com"`` blocks ``"sub.example.com"`` too).</span>
</span><span id="__span-0-23"><a id="__codelineno-0-23" name="__codelineno-0-23"></a><span class="sd">    :param block_ads: Block requests to ~3,500 known ad/tracking domains. Can be combined with ``blocked_domains``.</span>
</span><span id="__span-0-24"><a id="__codelineno-0-24" name="__codelineno-0-24"></a><span class="sd">    :param dns_over_https: Route DNS queries through Cloudflare's DNS-over-HTTPS to prevent DNS leaks when using proxies.</span>
</span><span id="__span-0-25"><a id="__codelineno-0-25" name="__codelineno-0-25"></a><span class="sd">    :param useragent: Pass a useragent string to be used. Otherwise the fetcher will generate a real Useragent of the same browser and use it.</span>
</span><span id="__span-0-26"><a id="__codelineno-0-26" name="__codelineno-0-26"></a><span class="sd">    :param cookies: Set cookies for the next request.</span>
</span><span id="__span-0-27"><a id="__codelineno-0-27" name="__codelineno-0-27"></a><span class="sd">    :param network_idle: Wait for the page until there are no network connections for at least 500 ms.</span>
</span><span id="__span-0-28"><a id="__codelineno-0-28" name="__codelineno-0-28"></a><span class="sd">    :param timeout: The timeout in milliseconds that is used in all operations and waits through the page. The default is 30,000</span>
</span><span id="__span-0-29"><a id="__codelineno-0-29" name="__codelineno-0-29"></a><span class="sd">    :param wait: The time (milliseconds) the fetcher will wait after everything finishes before closing the page and returning the ` Response ` object.</span>

</span><span id="__span-0-31"><a id="__codelineno-0-31" name="__codelineno-0-31"></a><span class="sd">    :param page_setup: A function that takes the `page` object, runs before navigation. Use it to register event listeners or routes that must be set up before the page loads.</span>

</span><span id="__span-0-33"><a id="__codelineno-0-33" name="__codelineno-0-33"></a><span class="sd">    :param init_script: An absolute path to a JavaScript file to be executed on page creation for all pages in this session.</span>
</span><span id="__span-0-34"><a id="__codelineno-0-34" name="__codelineno-0-34"></a><span class="sd">    :param locale: Specify user locale, for example, `en-GB`, `de-DE`, etc. Locale will affect navigator.language value, Accept-Language request header value as well as number and date formatting</span>
</span><span id="__span-0-35"><a id="__codelineno-0-35" name="__codelineno-0-35"></a><span class="sd">        rules. Defaults to the system default locale.</span>
</span><span id="__span-0-36"><a id="__codelineno-0-36" name="__codelineno-0-36"></a><span class="sd">    :param timezone_id: Changes the timezone of the browser. Defaults to the system timezone.</span>
</span><span id="__span-0-37"><a id="__codelineno-0-37" name="__codelineno-0-37"></a><span class="sd">    :param wait_selector_state: The state to wait for the selector given with `wait_selector`. The default state is `attached`.</span>
</span><span id="__span-0-38"><a id="__codelineno-0-38" name="__codelineno-0-38"></a><span class="sd">    :param solve_cloudflare: Solves all types of the Cloudflare's Turnstile/Interstitial challenges before returning the response to you.</span>
</span><span id="__span-0-39"><a id="__codelineno-0-39" name="__codelineno-0-39"></a><span class="sd">    :param real_chrome: If you have a Chrome browser installed on your device, enable this, and the Fetcher will launch an instance of your browser and use it.</span>
</span><span id="__span-0-40"><a id="__codelineno-0-40" name="__codelineno-0-40"></a><span class="sd">    :param hide_canvas: Add random noise to canvas operations to prevent fingerprinting.</span>
</span><span id="__span-0-41"><a id="__codelineno-0-41" name="__codelineno-0-41"></a><span class="sd">    :param block_webrtc: Forces WebRTC to respect proxy settings to prevent local IP address leak.</span>
</span><span id="__span-0-42"><a id="__codelineno-0-42" name="__codelineno-0-42"></a><span class="sd">    :param allow_webgl: Enabled by default. Disabling it disables WebGL and WebGL 2.0 support entirely. Disabling WebGL is not recommended as many WAFs now check if WebGL is enabled.</span>
</span><span id="__span-0-43"><a id="__codelineno-0-43" name="__codelineno-0-43"></a><span class="sd">    :param load_dom: Enabled by default, wait for all JavaScript on page(s) to fully load and execute.</span>
</span><span id="__span-0-44"><a id="__codelineno-0-44" name="__codelineno-0-44"></a><span class="sd">    :param cdp_url: Instead of launching a new browser instance, connect to this CDP URL to control real browsers through CDP.</span>
</span><span id="__span-0-45"><a id="__codelineno-0-45" name="__codelineno-0-45"></a><span class="sd">    :param google_search: Enabled by default, Scrapling will set a Google referer header.</span>

</span><span id="__span-0-47"><a id="__codelineno-0-47" name="__codelineno-0-47"></a><span class="sd">    :param proxy: The proxy to be used with requests, it can be a string or a dictionary with the keys 'server', 'username', and 'password' only.</span>
</span><span id="__span-0-48"><a id="__codelineno-0-48" name="__codelineno-0-48"></a><span class="sd">    :param user_data_dir: Path to a User Data Directory, which stores browser session data like cookies and local storage. The default is to create a temporary directory.</span>
</span><span id="__span-0-49"><a id="__codelineno-0-49" name="__codelineno-0-49"></a><span class="sd">    :param extra_flags: A list of additional browser flags to pass to the browser on launch.</span>
</span><span id="__span-0-50"><a id="__codelineno-0-50" name="__codelineno-0-50"></a><span class="sd">    :param selector_config: The arguments that will be passed in the end while creating the final Selector's class.</span>
</span><span id="__span-0-51"><a id="__codelineno-0-51" name="__codelineno-0-51"></a><span class="sd">    :param additional_args: Additional arguments to be passed to Playwright's context as additional settings, and it takes higher priority than Scrapling's settings.</span>

</span><span id="__span-0-54"><a id="__codelineno-0-54" name="__codelineno-0-54"></a>    <span class="n">selector_config</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">kwargs</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">get</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="s2">"selector_config"</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="p">{})</span> <span class="ow">or</span> <span class="n">kwargs</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">get</span><span class="p">(</span>
</span><span id="__span-0-55"><a id="__codelineno-0-55" name="__codelineno-0-55"></a>        <span class="s2">"custom_config"</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="p">{}</span>
</span><span id="__span-0-56"><a id="__codelineno-0-56" name="__codelineno-0-56"></a>    <span class="p">)</span>  <span class="c1"># Checking `custom_config` for backward compatibility</span>
</span><span id="__span-0-57"><a id="__codelineno-0-57" name="__codelineno-0-57"></a>    <span class="k">if</span> <span class="ow">not</span> <span class="nb">isinstance</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">selector_config</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="nb">dict</span><span class="p">):</span>
</span><span id="__span-0-58"><a id="__codelineno-0-58" name="__codelineno-0-58"></a>        <span class="k">raise</span> <span class="ne">TypeError</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="s2">"Argument `selector_config` must be a dictionary."</span><span class="p">)</span>

    <div class="doc doc-contents ">

        <p>Opens up a browser and do your request based on your chosen options below.</p>


<div class="md-typeset__scrollwrap"><div class="md-typeset__table"><table>
      <thead>
        <tr>
          <th><span class="doc-section-title">PARAMETER</span></th>
          <th><span>DESCRIPTION</span></th>
        </tr>
      </thead>
      <tbody>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>url</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>Target url.</p>
              </div>
              <p>
                  <span class="doc-param-annotation">
                    <b>TYPE:</b>
                      <code><a class="autorefs autorefs-external" href="https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str">str</a></code>
                  </span>
              </p>
            </td>
          </tr>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>headless</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>Run the browser in headless/hidden (default), or headful/visible mode.</p>
              </div>
              <p>
              </p>
            </td>
          </tr>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>disable_resources</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>Drop requests for unnecessary resources for a speed boost.
Requests dropped are of type <code>font</code>, <code>image</code>, <code>media</code>, <code>beacon</code>, <code>object</code>, <code>imageset</code>, <code>texttrack</code>, <code>websocket</code>, <code>csp_report</code>, and <code>stylesheet</code>.</p>
              </div>
              <p>
              </p>
            </td>
          </tr>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>blocked_domains</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>A set of domain names to block requests to. Subdomains are also matched (e.g., <code>"example.com"</code> blocks <code>"sub.example.com"</code> too).</p>
              </div>
              <p>
              </p>
            </td>
          </tr>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>block_ads</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>Block requests to ~3,500 known ad/tracking domains. Can be combined with <code>blocked_domains</code>.</p>
              </div>
              <p>
              </p>
            </td>
          </tr>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>dns_over_https</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>Route DNS queries through Cloudflare's DNS-over-HTTPS to prevent DNS leaks when using proxies.</p>
              </div>
              <p>
              </p>
            </td>
          </tr>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>useragent</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>Pass a useragent string to be used. Otherwise the fetcher will generate a real Useragent of the same browser and use it.</p>
              </div>
              <p>
              </p>
            </td>
          </tr>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>cookies</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>Set cookies for the next request.</p>
              </div>
              <p>
              </p>
            </td>
          </tr>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>network_idle</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>Wait for the page until there are no network connections for at least 500 ms.</p>
              </div>
              <p>
              </p>
            </td>
          </tr>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>timeout</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>The timeout in milliseconds that is used in all operations and waits through the page. The default is 30,000</p>
              </div>
              <p>
              </p>
            </td>
          </tr>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>wait</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>The time (milliseconds) the fetcher will wait after everything finishes before closing the page and returning the <code>Response</code> object.</p>
              </div>
              <p>
              </p>
            </td>
          </tr>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>page_action</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>Added for automation. A function that takes the <code>page</code> object, runs after navigation, and does the automation you need.</p>
              </div>
              <p>
              </p>
            </td>
          </tr>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>page_setup</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>A function that takes the <code>page</code> object, runs before navigation. Use it to register event listeners or routes that must be set up before the page loads.</p>
              </div>
              <p>
              </p>
            </td>
          </tr>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>wait_selector</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>Wait for a specific CSS selector to be in a specific state.</p>
              </div>
              <p>
              </p>
            </td>
          </tr>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>init_script</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>An absolute path to a JavaScript file to be executed on page creation for all pages in this session.</p>
              </div>
              <p>
              </p>
            </td>
          </tr>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>locale</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>Specify user locale, for example, <code>en-GB</code>, <code>de-DE</code>, etc. Locale will affect navigator.language value, Accept-Language request header value as well as number and date formatting
rules. Defaults to the system default locale.</p>
              </div>
              <p>
              </p>
            </td>
          </tr>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>timezone_id</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>Changes the timezone of the browser. Defaults to the system timezone.</p>
              </div>
              <p>
              </p>
            </td>
          </tr>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>wait_selector_state</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>The state to wait for the selector given with <code>wait_selector</code>. The default state is <code>attached</code>.</p>
              </div>
              <p>
              </p>
            </td>
          </tr>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>solve_cloudflare</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>Solves all types of the Cloudflare's Turnstile/Interstitial challenges before returning the response to you.</p>
              </div>
              <p>
              </p>
            </td>
          </tr>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>real_chrome</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>If you have a Chrome browser installed on your device, enable this, and the Fetcher will launch an instance of your browser and use it.</p>
              </div>
              <p>
              </p>
            </td>
          </tr>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>hide_canvas</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>Add random noise to canvas operations to prevent fingerprinting.</p>
              </div>
              <p>
              </p>
            </td>
          </tr>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>block_webrtc</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>Forces WebRTC to respect proxy settings to prevent local IP address leak.</p>
              </div>
              <p>
              </p>
            </td>
          </tr>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>allow_webgl</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>Enabled by default. Disabling it disables WebGL and WebGL 2.0 support entirely. Disabling WebGL is not recommended as many WAFs now check if WebGL is enabled.</p>
              </div>
              <p>
              </p>
            </td>
          </tr>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>load_dom</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>Enabled by default, wait for all JavaScript on page(s) to fully load and execute.</p>
              </div>
              <p>
              </p>
            </td>
          </tr>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>cdp_url</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>Instead of launching a new browser instance, connect to this CDP URL to control real browsers through CDP.</p>
              </div>
              <p>
              </p>
            </td>
          </tr>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>google_search</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>Enabled by default, Scrapling will set a Google referer header.</p>
              </div>
              <p>
              </p>
            </td>
          </tr>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>extra_headers</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>A dictionary of extra headers to add to the request. <em>The referer set by <code>google_search</code> takes priority over the referer set here if used together.</em></p>
              </div>
              <p>
              </p>
            </td>
          </tr>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>proxy</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>The proxy to be used with requests, it can be a string or a dictionary with the keys 'server', 'username', and 'password' only.</p>
              </div>
              <p>
              </p>
            </td>
          </tr>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>user_data_dir</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>Path to a User Data Directory, which stores browser session data like cookies and local storage. The default is to create a temporary directory.</p>
              </div>
              <p>
              </p>
            </td>
          </tr>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>extra_flags</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>A list of additional browser flags to pass to the browser on launch.</p>
              </div>
              <p>
              </p>
            </td>
          </tr>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>selector_config</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>The arguments that will be passed in the end while creating the final Selector's class.</p>
              </div>
              <p>
              </p>
            </td>
          </tr>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>additional_args</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>Additional arguments to be passed to Playwright's context as additional settings, and it takes higher priority than Scrapling's settings.</p>
              </div>
              <p>
              </p>

</span><span id="__span-0-68"><a id="__codelineno-0-68" name="__codelineno-0-68"></a><span class="sd">    Opens up a browser and do your request based on your chosen options below.</span>

</span><span id="__span-0-72"><a id="__codelineno-0-72" name="__codelineno-0-72"></a><span class="sd">    :param disable_resources: Drop requests for unnecessary resources for a speed boost.</span>

</span><span id="__span-0-74"><a id="__codelineno-0-74" name="__codelineno-0-74"></a><span class="sd">    :param blocked_domains: A set of domain names to block requests to. Subdomains are also matched (e.g., ``"example.com"`` blocks ``"sub.example.com"`` too).</span>
</span><span id="__span-0-75"><a id="__codelineno-0-75" name="__codelineno-0-75"></a><span class="sd">    :param block_ads: Block requests to ~3,500 known ad/tracking domains. Can be combined with ``blocked_domains``.</span>

</span><span id="__span-0-80"><a id="__codelineno-0-80" name="__codelineno-0-80"></a><span class="sd">    :param timeout: The timeout in milliseconds that is used in all operations and waits through the page. The default is 30,000</span>
</span><span id="__span-0-81"><a id="__codelineno-0-81" name="__codelineno-0-81"></a><span class="sd">    :param wait: The time (milliseconds) the fetcher will wait after everything finishes before closing the page and returning the ` Response ` object.</span>
</span><span id="__span-0-82"><a id="__codelineno-0-82" name="__codelineno-0-82"></a><span class="sd">    :param page_action: Added for automation. A function that takes the `page` object, runs after navigation, and does the automation you need.</span>
</span><span id="__span-0-83"><a id="__codelineno-0-83" name="__codelineno-0-83"></a><span class="sd">    :param page_setup: A function that takes the `page` object, runs before navigation. Use it to register event listeners or routes that must be set up before the page loads.</span>
</span><span id="__span-0-84"><a id="__codelineno-0-84" name="__codelineno-0-84"></a><span class="sd">    :param wait_selector: Wait for a specific CSS selector to be in a specific state.</span>
</span><span id="__span-0-85"><a id="__codelineno-0-85" name="__codelineno-0-85"></a><span class="sd">    :param init_script: An absolute path to a JavaScript file to be executed on page creation for all pages in this session.</span>
</span><span id="__span-0-86"><a id="__codelineno-0-86" name="__codelineno-0-86"></a><span class="sd">    :param locale: Specify user locale, for example, `en-GB`, `de-DE`, etc. Locale will affect navigator.language value, Accept-Language request header value as well as number and date formatting</span>

</span><span id="__span-0-88"><a id="__codelineno-0-88" name="__codelineno-0-88"></a><span class="sd">    :param timezone_id: Changes the timezone of the browser. Defaults to the system timezone.</span>
</span><span id="__span-0-89"><a id="__codelineno-0-89" name="__codelineno-0-89"></a><span class="sd">    :param wait_selector_state: The state to wait for the selector given with `wait_selector`. The default state is `attached`.</span>
</span><span id="__span-0-90"><a id="__codelineno-0-90" name="__codelineno-0-90"></a><span class="sd">    :param solve_cloudflare: Solves all types of the Cloudflare's Turnstile/Interstitial challenges before returning the response to you.</span>
</span><span id="__span-0-91"><a id="__codelineno-0-91" name="__codelineno-0-91"></a><span class="sd">    :param real_chrome: If you have a Chrome browser installed on your device, enable this, and the Fetcher will launch an instance of your browser and use it.</span>
</span><span id="__span-0-92"><a id="__codelineno-0-92" name="__codelineno-0-92"></a><span class="sd">    :param hide_canvas: Add random noise to canvas operations to prevent fingerprinting.</span>

    <div class="doc doc-contents first">



        <p>A factory context manager that provides configured Fetcher sessions.</p>
<p>When this manager is used in a 'with' or 'async with' block,
it yields a new session configured with the manager's defaults.
A single instance of this manager should ideally be used for one active
session at a time (or sequentially). Re-entering a context with the
same manager instance while a session is already active is disallowed.</p>

        


<div class="md-typeset__scrollwrap"><div class="md-typeset__table"><table>

      </thead>
      <tbody>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>impersonate</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>Browser version to impersonate. Can be a single browser string or a list of browser strings for random selection. (Default: latest available Chrome version)</p>
              </div>
              <p>
                  <span class="doc-param-annotation">
                    <b>TYPE:</b>
                      <code><span title="scrapling.engines._browsers._types.ImpersonateType">ImpersonateType</span></code>
                  </span>
                  <span class="doc-param-default">
                    <b>DEFAULT:</b>
                      <code>'chrome'</code>
                  </span>
              </p>
            </td>
          </tr>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>http3</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>Whether to use HTTP3. Defaults to False. It might be problematic if used it with <code>impersonate</code>.</p>
              </div>
              <p>
                  <span class="doc-param-annotation">
                    <b>TYPE:</b>
