<h3 id="scrapling.fetchers.DynamicSession.fetch" class="doc doc-heading">
<code class="doc-symbol doc-symbol-heading doc-symbol-method"></code>            <span class="doc doc-object-name doc-function-name">fetch</span>


<a href="https://scrapling.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api-reference/fetchers.html#scrapling.fetchers.DynamicSession.fetch" class="headerlink" title="Permanent link">¶</a></h3>
<div class="language-python doc-signature highlight"><pre id="__code_173"><span></span><nav class="md-code__nav"><button class="md-code__button" title="Copy to clipboard" data-clipboard-target="#__code_173 > code" data-md-type="copy"></button></nav><code class="md-code__content"><span id="__span-0-1"><a id="__codelineno-0-1" name="__codelineno-0-1" href="https://scrapling.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api-reference/fetchers.html#__codelineno-0-1"></a><span class="nf">fetch</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">url</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="o">**</span><span class="n">kwargs</span><span class="p">)</span>
</span></code></pre></div>

    <div class="doc doc-contents ">

        <p>Opens up the browser and do your request based on your chosen options.</p>


<div class="md-typeset__scrollwrap"><div class="md-typeset__table"><table>
      <thead>
        <tr>
          <th><span class="doc-section-title">PARAMETER</span></th>
          <th><span>DESCRIPTION</span></th>
        </tr>
      </thead>
      <tbody>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>url</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>The Target url.</p>
              </div>
              <p>
                  <span class="doc-param-annotation">
                    <b>TYPE:</b>
                      <code><a class="autorefs autorefs-external" href="https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str">str</a></code>
                  </span>
              </p>
            </td>
          </tr>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>google_search</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>Enabled by default, Scrapling will set a Google referer header.</p>
              </div>
              <p>
              </p>
            </td>
          </tr>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>timeout</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>The timeout in milliseconds that is used in all operations and waits through the page. The default is 30,000</p>
              </div>
              <p>
              </p>
            </td>
          </tr>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>wait</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>The time (milliseconds) the fetcher will wait after everything finishes before closing the page and returning the <code>Response</code> object.</p>
              </div>
              <p>
              </p>
            </td>
          </tr>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>page_action</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>Added for automation. A function that takes the <code>page</code> object, runs after navigation, and does the automation you need.</p>
              </div>
              <p>
              </p>
            </td>
          </tr>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>page_setup</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>A function that takes the <code>page</code> object, runs before navigation. Use it to register event listeners or routes that must be set up before the page loads.</p>
              </div>
              <p>
              </p>
            </td>
          </tr>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>extra_headers</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>A dictionary of extra headers to add to the request. <em>The referer set by <code>google_search</code> takes priority over the referer set here if used together.</em></p>
              </div>
              <p>
              </p>
            </td>
          </tr>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>disable_resources</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>Drop requests for unnecessary resources for a speed boost.
Requests dropped are of type <code>font</code>, <code>image</code>, <code>media</code>, <code>beacon</code>, <code>object</code>, <code>imageset</code>, <code>texttrack</code>, <code>websocket</code>, <code>csp_report</code>, and <code>stylesheet</code>.</p>
              </div>
              <p>
              </p>
            </td>
          </tr>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>blocked_domains</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>A set of domain names to block requests to. Subdomains are also matched (e.g., <code>"example.com"</code> blocks <code>"sub.example.com"</code> too).</p>
              </div>
              <p>
              </p>
            </td>
          </tr>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>wait_selector</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>Wait for a specific CSS selector to be in a specific state.</p>
              </div>
              <p>
              </p>
            </td>
          </tr>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>wait_selector_state</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>The state to wait for the selector given with <code>wait_selector</code>. The default state is <code>attached</code>.</p>
              </div>
              <p>
              </p>
            </td>
          </tr>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>network_idle</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>Wait for the page until there are no network connections for at least 500 ms.</p>
              </div>
              <p>
              </p>
            </td>
          </tr>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>load_dom</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>Enabled by default, wait for all JavaScript on page(s) to fully load and execute.</p>
              </div>
              <p>
              </p>
            </td>
          </tr>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>selector_config</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>The arguments that will be passed in the end while creating the final Selector's class.</p>
              </div>
              <p>
              </p>
            </td>
          </tr>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>proxy</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>Static proxy to override rotator and session proxy. A new browser context will be created and used with it.</p>
              </div>
              <p>
              </p>
            </td>
          </tr>
      </tbody>
    </table></div></div>

</span><span id="__span-0-103"><a id="__codelineno-0-103" name="__codelineno-0-103"></a><span class="w">    </span><span class="sd">"""Opens up the browser and do your request based on your chosen options.</span>
</span><span id="__span-0-104"><a id="__codelineno-0-104" name="__codelineno-0-104"></a>
</span><span id="__span-0-105"><a id="__codelineno-0-105" name="__codelineno-0-105"></a><span class="sd">    :param url: The Target url.</span>
</span><span id="__span-0-106"><a id="__codelineno-0-106" name="__codelineno-0-106"></a><span class="sd">    :param google_search: Enabled by default, Scrapling will set a Google referer header.</span>
</span><span id="__span-0-107"><a id="__codelineno-0-107" name="__codelineno-0-107"></a><span class="sd">    :param timeout: The timeout in milliseconds that is used in all operations and waits through the page. The default is 30,000</span>
</span><span id="__span-0-108"><a id="__codelineno-0-108" name="__codelineno-0-108"></a><span class="sd">    :param wait: The time (milliseconds) the fetcher will wait after everything finishes before closing the page and returning the ` Response ` object.</span>
</span><span id="__span-0-109"><a id="__codelineno-0-109" name="__codelineno-0-109"></a><span class="sd">    :param page_action: Added for automation. A function that takes the `page` object, runs after navigation, and does the automation you need.</span>
</span><span id="__span-0-110"><a id="__codelineno-0-110" name="__codelineno-0-110"></a><span class="sd">    :param page_setup: A function that takes the `page` object, runs before navigation. Use it to register event listeners or routes that must be set up before the page loads.</span>
</span><span id="__span-0-111"><a id="__codelineno-0-111" name="__codelineno-0-111"></a><span class="sd">    :param extra_headers: A dictionary of extra headers to add to the request. _The referer set by `google_search` takes priority over the referer set here if used together._</span>
</span><span id="__span-0-112"><a id="__codelineno-0-112" name="__codelineno-0-112"></a><span class="sd">    :param disable_resources: Drop requests for unnecessary resources for a speed boost.</span>
</span><span id="__span-0-113"><a id="__codelineno-0-113" name="__codelineno-0-113"></a><span class="sd">        Requests dropped are of type `font`, `image`, `media`, `beacon`, `object`, `imageset`, `texttrack`, `websocket`, `csp_report`, and `stylesheet`.</span>
</span><span id="__span-0-114"><a id="__codelineno-0-114" name="__codelineno-0-114"></a><span class="sd">    :param blocked_domains: A set of domain names to block requests to. Subdomains are also matched (e.g., ``"example.com"`` blocks ``"sub.example.com"`` too).</span>
</span><span id="__span-0-115"><a id="__codelineno-0-115" name="__codelineno-0-115"></a><span class="sd">    :param wait_selector: Wait for a specific CSS selector to be in a specific state.</span>
</span><span id="__span-0-116"><a id="__codelineno-0-116" name="__codelineno-0-116"></a><span class="sd">    :param wait_selector_state: The state to wait for the selector given with `wait_selector`. The default state is `attached`.</span>
</span><span id="__span-0-117"><a id="__codelineno-0-117" name="__codelineno-0-117"></a><span class="sd">    :param network_idle: Wait for the page until there are no network connections for at least 500 ms.</span>
</span><span id="__span-0-118"><a id="__codelineno-0-118" name="__codelineno-0-118"></a><span class="sd">    :param load_dom: Enabled by default, wait for all JavaScript on page(s) to fully load and execute.</span>
</span><span id="__span-0-119"><a id="__codelineno-0-119" name="__codelineno-0-119"></a><span class="sd">    :param selector_config: The arguments that will be passed in the end while creating the final Selector's class.</span>
</span><span id="__span-0-120"><a id="__codelineno-0-120" name="__codelineno-0-120"></a><span class="sd">    :param proxy: Static proxy to override rotator and session proxy. A new browser context will be created and used with it.</span>
</span><span id="__span-0-121"><a id="__codelineno-0-121" name="__codelineno-0-121"></a><span class="sd">    :return: A `Response` object.</span>

</span><span id="__span-0-123"><a id="__codelineno-0-123" name="__codelineno-0-123"></a>    <span class="n">static_proxy</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">kwargs</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">pop</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="s2">"proxy"</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="kc">None</span><span class="p">)</span>

<h2 id="scrapling.fetchers.AsyncDynamicSession" class="doc doc-heading">

              Bases: <code><span title="scrapling.engines._browsers._base.AsyncSession">AsyncSession</span></code>, <code><span title="scrapling.engines._browsers._base.DynamicSessionMixin">DynamicSessionMixin</span></code></p>

            <div style="display: none">

            <script>
              var diagram = document.getElementById('mermaid-diagram-scrapling.fetchers.AsyncDynamicSession');
              diagram.innerHTML = diagram.innerHTML.replace(/click ([\w.]+) href "" "\1"/g, function(match, nodeID, offset) {
                try {
                  const link = document.getElementById("mermaid-link-" + nodeID).href;

        <p>An async Browser session manager with page pooling, it's using a persistent browser Context by default with a temporary user profile directory.</p>

        <p>A Browser session manager with page pooling</p>


<div class="md-typeset__scrollwrap"><div class="md-typeset__table"><table>
      <thead>
        <tr>
          <th><span class="doc-section-title">PARAMETER</span></th>
          <th><span>DESCRIPTION</span></th>
        </tr>
      </thead>
      <tbody>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>headless</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>Run the browser in headless/hidden (default), or headful/visible mode.</p>
              </div>
              <p>
              </p>
            </td>
          </tr>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>disable_resources</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>Drop requests for unnecessary resources for a speed boost.
Requests dropped are of type <code>font</code>, <code>image</code>, <code>media</code>, <code>beacon</code>, <code>object</code>, <code>imageset</code>, <code>texttrack</code>, <code>websocket</code>, <code>csp_report</code>, and <code>stylesheet</code>.</p>
              </div>
              <p>
              </p>
            </td>
          </tr>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>blocked_domains</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>A set of domain names to block requests to. Subdomains are also matched (e.g., <code>"example.com"</code> blocks <code>"sub.example.com"</code> too).</p>
              </div>
              <p>
              </p>
            </td>
          </tr>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>useragent</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>Pass a useragent string to be used. Otherwise the fetcher will generate a real Useragent of the same browser and use it.</p>
              </div>
              <p>
              </p>
            </td>
          </tr>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>cookies</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>Set cookies for the next request.</p>
              </div>
              <p>
              </p>
            </td>
          </tr>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>network_idle</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>Wait for the page until there are no network connections for at least 500 ms.</p>
              </div>
              <p>
              </p>
            </td>
          </tr>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>load_dom</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>Enabled by default, wait for all JavaScript on page(s) to fully load and execute.</p>
              </div>
              <p>
              </p>
            </td>
          </tr>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>timeout</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>The timeout in milliseconds that is used in all operations and waits through the page. The default is 30,000</p>
              </div>
              <p>
              </p>
            </td>
          </tr>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>wait</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>The time (milliseconds) the fetcher will wait after everything finishes before closing the page and returning the <code>Response</code> object.</p>
              </div>
              <p>
              </p>
            </td>
          </tr>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>page_action</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>Added for automation. A function that takes the <code>page</code> object, runs after navigation, and does the automation you need.</p>
              </div>
              <p>
              </p>
            </td>
          </tr>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>page_setup</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>A function that takes the <code>page</code> object, runs before navigation. Use it to register event listeners or routes that must be set up before the page loads.</p>
              </div>
              <p>
              </p>
            </td>
          </tr>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>wait_selector</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>Wait for a specific CSS selector to be in a specific state.</p>
              </div>
              <p>
              </p>
            </td>
          </tr>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>init_script</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>An absolute path to a JavaScript file to be executed on page creation for all pages in this session.</p>
              </div>
              <p>
              </p>
            </td>
          </tr>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>locale</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>Specify user locale, for example, <code>en-GB</code>, <code>de-DE</code>, etc. Locale will affect navigator.language value, Accept-Language request header value as well as number and date formatting
rules. Defaults to the system default locale.</p>
              </div>
              <p>
              </p>
            </td>
          </tr>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>timezone_id</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>Changes the timezone of the browser. Defaults to the system timezone.</p>
              </div>
              <p>
              </p>
            </td>
          </tr>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>wait_selector_state</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>The state to wait for the selector given with <code>wait_selector</code>. The default state is <code>attached</code>.</p>
              </div>
              <p>
              </p>
            </td>
          </tr>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>real_chrome</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>If you have a Chrome browser installed on your device, enable this, and the Fetcher will launch an instance of your browser and use it.</p>
              </div>
              <p>
              </p>
            </td>
          </tr>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>cdp_url</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>Instead of launching a new browser instance, connect to this CDP URL to control real browsers through CDP.</p>
              </div>
              <p>
              </p>

            <td>

            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">

              <p>

          </tr>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>

            </td>

              <p>
              </p>

          </tr>

</span><span id="__span-0-227"><a id="__codelineno-0-227" name="__codelineno-0-227"></a><span class="w">    </span><span class="sd">"""A Browser session manager with page pooling</span>
</span><span id="__span-0-228"><a id="__codelineno-0-228" name="__codelineno-0-228"></a>
</span><span id="__span-0-229"><a id="__codelineno-0-229" name="__codelineno-0-229"></a><span class="sd">    :param headless: Run the browser in headless/hidden (default), or headful/visible mode.</span>
</span><span id="__span-0-230"><a id="__codelineno-0-230" name="__codelineno-0-230"></a><span class="sd">    :param disable_resources: Drop requests for unnecessary resources for a speed boost.</span>
</span><span id="__span-0-231"><a id="__codelineno-0-231" name="__codelineno-0-231"></a><span class="sd">        Requests dropped are of type `font`, `image`, `media`, `beacon`, `object`, `imageset`, `texttrack`, `websocket`, `csp_report`, and `stylesheet`.</span>
</span><span id="__span-0-232"><a id="__codelineno-0-232" name="__codelineno-0-232"></a><span class="sd">    :param blocked_domains: A set of domain names to block requests to. Subdomains are also matched (e.g., ``"example.com"`` blocks ``"sub.example.com"`` too).</span>
</span><span id="__span-0-233"><a id="__codelineno-0-233" name="__codelineno-0-233"></a><span class="sd">    :param useragent: Pass a useragent string to be used. Otherwise the fetcher will generate a real Useragent of the same browser and use it.</span>
</span><span id="__span-0-234"><a id="__codelineno-0-234" name="__codelineno-0-234"></a><span class="sd">    :param cookies: Set cookies for the next request.</span>
</span><span id="__span-0-235"><a id="__codelineno-0-235" name="__codelineno-0-235"></a><span class="sd">    :param network_idle: Wait for the page until there are no network connections for at least 500 ms.</span>
</span><span id="__span-0-236"><a id="__codelineno-0-236" name="__codelineno-0-236"></a><span class="sd">    :param load_dom: Enabled by default, wait for all JavaScript on page(s) to fully load and execute.</span>
</span><span id="__span-0-237"><a id="__codelineno-0-237" name="__codelineno-0-237"></a><span class="sd">    :param timeout: The timeout in milliseconds that is used in all operations and waits through the page. The default is 30,000</span>
</span><span id="__span-0-238"><a id="__codelineno-0-238" name="__codelineno-0-238"></a><span class="sd">    :param wait: The time (milliseconds) the fetcher will wait after everything finishes before closing the page and returning the ` Response ` object.</span>
</span><span id="__span-0-239"><a id="__codelineno-0-239" name="__codelineno-0-239"></a><span class="sd">    :param page_action: Added for automation. A function that takes the `page` object, runs after navigation, and does the automation you need.</span>
</span><span id="__span-0-240"><a id="__codelineno-0-240" name="__codelineno-0-240"></a><span class="sd">    :param page_setup: A function that takes the `page` object, runs before navigation. Use it to register event listeners or routes that must be set up before the page loads.</span>
</span><span id="__span-0-241"><a id="__codelineno-0-241" name="__codelineno-0-241"></a><span class="sd">    :param wait_selector: Wait for a specific CSS selector to be in a specific state.</span>
</span><span id="__span-0-242"><a id="__codelineno-0-242" name="__codelineno-0-242"></a><span class="sd">    :param init_script: An absolute path to a JavaScript file to be executed on page creation for all pages in this session.</span>
</span><span id="__span-0-243"><a id="__codelineno-0-243" name="__codelineno-0-243"></a><span class="sd">    :param locale: Specify user locale, for example, `en-GB`, `de-DE`, etc. Locale will affect navigator.language value, Accept-Language request header value as well as number and date formatting</span>
</span><span id="__span-0-244"><a id="__codelineno-0-244" name="__codelineno-0-244"></a><span class="sd">        rules. Defaults to the system default locale.</span>
</span><span id="__span-0-245"><a id="__codelineno-0-245" name="__codelineno-0-245"></a><span class="sd">    :param timezone_id: Changes the timezone of the browser. Defaults to the system timezone.</span>
</span><span id="__span-0-246"><a id="__codelineno-0-246" name="__codelineno-0-246"></a><span class="sd">    :param wait_selector_state: The state to wait for the selector given with `wait_selector`. The default state is `attached`.</span>
</span><span id="__span-0-247"><a id="__codelineno-0-247" name="__codelineno-0-247"></a><span class="sd">    :param real_chrome: If you have a Chrome browser installed on your device, enable this, and the Fetcher will launch an instance of your browser and use it.</span>
</span><span id="__span-0-248"><a id="__codelineno-0-248" name="__codelineno-0-248"></a><span class="sd">    :param cdp_url: Instead of launching a new browser instance, connect to this CDP URL to control real browsers through CDP.</span>
</span><span id="__span-0-249"><a id="__codelineno-0-249" name="__codelineno-0-249"></a><span class="sd">    :param google_search: Enabled by default, Scrapling will set a Google referer header.</span>
</span><span id="__span-0-250"><a id="__codelineno-0-250" name="__codelineno-0-250"></a><span class="sd">    :param extra_headers: A dictionary of extra headers to add to the request. _The referer set by `google_search` takes priority over the referer set here if used together._</span>
</span><span id="__span-0-251"><a id="__codelineno-0-251" name="__codelineno-0-251"></a><span class="sd">    :param proxy: The proxy to be used with requests, it can be a string or a dictionary with the keys 'server', 'username', and 'password' only.</span>
</span><span id="__span-0-252"><a id="__codelineno-0-252" name="__codelineno-0-252"></a><span class="sd">    :param max_pages: The maximum number of tabs to be opened at the same time. It will be used in rotation through a PagePool.</span>
</span><span id="__span-0-253"><a id="__codelineno-0-253" name="__codelineno-0-253"></a><span class="sd">    :param user_data_dir: Path to a User Data Directory, which stores browser session data like cookies and local storage. The default is to create a temporary directory.</span>
</span><span id="__span-0-254"><a id="__codelineno-0-254" name="__codelineno-0-254"></a><span class="sd">    :param extra_flags: A list of additional browser flags to pass to the browser on launch.</span>
</span><span id="__span-0-255"><a id="__codelineno-0-255" name="__codelineno-0-255"></a><span class="sd">    :param selector_config: The arguments that will be passed in the end while creating the final Selector's class.</span>
</span><span id="__span-0-256"><a id="__codelineno-0-256" name="__codelineno-0-256"></a><span class="sd">    :param additional_args: Additional arguments to be passed to Playwright's context as additional settings, and it takes higher priority than Scrapling's settings.</span>
</span><span id="__span-0-257"><a id="__codelineno-0-257" name="__codelineno-0-257"></a><span class="sd">    """</span>
</span><span id="__span-0-258"><a id="__codelineno-0-258" name="__codelineno-0-258"></a>    <span class="bp">self</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">__validate__</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="o">**</span><span class="n">kwargs</span><span class="p">)</span>
</span><span id="__span-0-259"><a id="__codelineno-0-259" name="__codelineno-0-259"></a>    <span class="nb">super</span><span class="p">()</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="fm">__init__</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">max_pages</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="bp">self</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">_config</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">max_pages</span><span class="p">)</span>
</span></code></pre></div></td></tr></tbody></table></div>

<h3 id="scrapling.fetchers.AsyncDynamicSession.fetch" class="doc doc-heading">
<code class="doc-symbol doc-symbol-heading doc-symbol-method"></code>            <span class="doc doc-object-name doc-function-name">fetch</span>


  <span class="doc doc-labels">
      <small class="doc doc-label doc-label-async"><code>async</code></small>
  </span>

<a href="https://scrapling.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api-reference/fetchers.html#scrapling.fetchers.AsyncDynamicSession.fetch" class="headerlink" title="Permanent link">¶</a></h3>
<div class="language-python doc-signature highlight"><pre id="__code_199"><span></span><nav class="md-code__nav"><button class="md-code__button" title="Copy to clipboard" data-clipboard-target="#__code_199 > code" data-md-type="copy"></button></nav><code class="md-code__content"><span id="__span-0-1"><a id="__codelineno-0-1" name="__codelineno-0-1" href="https://scrapling.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api-reference/fetchers.html#__codelineno-0-1"></a><span class="nf">fetch</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">url</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="o">**</span><span class="n">kwargs</span><span class="p">)</span>
</span></code></pre></div>

    <div class="doc doc-contents ">

        <p>Opens up the browser and do your request based on your chosen options.</p>


<div class="md-typeset__scrollwrap"><div class="md-typeset__table"><table>
      <thead>
        <tr>
          <th><span class="doc-section-title">PARAMETER</span></th>
          <th><span>DESCRIPTION</span></th>
        </tr>
      </thead>
      <tbody>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>url</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>The Target url.</p>
              </div>
              <p>
                  <span class="doc-param-annotation">
                    <b>TYPE:</b>
                      <code><a class="autorefs autorefs-external" href="https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str">str</a></code>
                  </span>
              </p>
            </td>
          </tr>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>google_search</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>Enabled by default, Scrapling will set a Google referer header.</p>
              </div>
              <p>
              </p>
            </td>
          </tr>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>timeout</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>The timeout in milliseconds that is used in all operations and waits through the page. The default is 30,000</p>
              </div>
              <p>
              </p>
            </td>
          </tr>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>wait</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>The time (milliseconds) the fetcher will wait after everything finishes before closing the page and returning the <code>Response</code> object.</p>
              </div>
              <p>
              </p>
            </td>
          </tr>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>page_action</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>Added for automation. A function that takes the <code>page</code> object, runs after navigation, and does the automation you need.</p>
              </div>
              <p>
              </p>
            </td>
          </tr>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>page_setup</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>A function that takes the <code>page</code> object, runs before navigation. Use it to register event listeners or routes that must be set up before the page loads.</p>
              </div>
              <p>
              </p>
            </td>
          </tr>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>extra_headers</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>A dictionary of extra headers to add to the request. <em>The referer set by <code>google_search</code> takes priority over the referer set here if used together.</em></p>
              </div>
              <p>
              </p>
            </td>
          </tr>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>disable_resources</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>Drop requests for unnecessary resources for a speed boost.
Requests dropped are of type <code>font</code>, <code>image</code>, <code>media</code>, <code>beacon</code>, <code>object</code>, <code>imageset</code>, <code>texttrack</code>, <code>websocket</code>, <code>csp_report</code>, and <code>stylesheet</code>.</p>
              </div>
              <p>
              </p>
            </td>
          </tr>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>blocked_domains</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>A set of domain names to block requests to. Subdomains are also matched (e.g., <code>"example.com"</code> blocks <code>"sub.example.com"</code> too).</p>
              </div>
              <p>
              </p>
            </td>
          </tr>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>wait_selector</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>Wait for a specific CSS selector to be in a specific state.</p>
              </div>
              <p>
              </p>
            </td>
          </tr>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>wait_selector_state</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>The state to wait for the selector given with <code>wait_selector</code>. The default state is <code>attached</code>.</p>
              </div>
              <p>
              </p>
            </td>
          </tr>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>network_idle</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>Wait for the page until there are no network connections for at least 500 ms.</p>
              </div>
              <p>
              </p>
            </td>
          </tr>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>load_dom</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>Enabled by default, wait for all JavaScript on page(s) to fully load and execute.</p>
              </div>
              <p>
              </p>
            </td>
          </tr>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>selector_config</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>The arguments that will be passed in the end while creating the final Selector's class.</p>
              </div>
              <p>
              </p>
            </td>
          </tr>
          <tr class="doc-section-item">
            <td>
                <code>proxy</code>
            </td>
            <td class="doc-param-details">
              <div class="doc-md-description">
                <p>Static proxy to override rotator and session proxy. A new browser context will be created and used with it.</p>
              </div>
              <p>
              </p>
            </td>
          </tr>
      </tbody>
    </table></div></div>

</span><span id="__span-0-291"><a id="__codelineno-0-291" name="__codelineno-0-291"></a><span class="w">    </span><span class="sd">"""Opens up the browser and do your request based on your chosen options.</span>
</span><span id="__span-0-292"><a id="__codelineno-0-292" name="__codelineno-0-292"></a>
</span><span id="__span-0-293"><a id="__codelineno-0-293" name="__codelineno-0-293"></a><span class="sd">    :param url: The Target url.</span>
</span><span id="__span-0-294"><a id="__codelineno-0-294" name="__codelineno-0-294"></a><span class="sd">    :param google_search: Enabled by default, Scrapling will set a Google referer header.</span>
</span><span id="__span-0-295"><a id="__codelineno-0-295" name="__codelineno-0-295"></a><span class="sd">    :param timeout: The timeout in milliseconds that is used in all operations and waits through the page. The default is 30,000</span>
</span><span id="__span-0-296"><a id="__codelineno-0-296" name="__codelineno-0-296"></a><span class="sd">    :param wait: The time (milliseconds) the fetcher will wait after everything finishes before closing the page and returning the ` Response ` object.</span>
</span><span id="__span-0-297"><a id="__codelineno-0-297" name="__codelineno-0-297"></a><span class="sd">    :param page_action: Added for automation. A function that takes the `page` object, runs after navigation, and does the automation you need.</span>
</span><span id="__span-0-298"><a id="__codelineno-0-298" name="__codelineno-0-298"></a><span class="sd">    :param page_setup: A function that takes the `page` object, runs before navigation. Use it to register event listeners or routes that must be set up before the page loads.</span>
</span><span id="__span-0-299"><a id="__codelineno-0-299" name="__codelineno-0-299"></a><span class="sd">    :param extra_headers: A dictionary of extra headers to add to the request. _The referer set by `google_search` takes priority over the referer set here if used together._</span>
</span><span id="__span-0-300"><a id="__codelineno-0-300" name="__codelineno-0-300"></a><span class="sd">    :param disable_resources: Drop requests for unnecessary resources for a speed boost.</span>
</span><span id="__span-0-301"><a id="__codelineno-0-301" name="__codelineno-0-301"></a><span class="sd">        Requests dropped are of type `font`, `image`, `media`, `beacon`, `object`, `imageset`, `texttrack`, `websocket`, `csp_report`, and `stylesheet`.</span>
</span><span id="__span-0-302"><a id="__codelineno-0-302" name="__codelineno-0-302"></a><span class="sd">    :param blocked_domains: A set of domain names to block requests to. Subdomains are also matched (e.g., ``"example.com"`` blocks ``"sub.example.com"`` too).</span>
</span><span id="__span-0-303"><a id="__codelineno-0-303" name="__codelineno-0-303"></a><span class="sd">    :param wait_selector: Wait for a specific CSS selector to be in a specific state.</span>
</span><span id="__span-0-304"><a id="__codelineno-0-304" name="__codelineno-0-304"></a><span class="sd">    :param wait_selector_state: The state to wait for the selector given with `wait_selector`. The default state is `attached`.</span>
</span><span id="__span-0-305"><a id="__codelineno-0-305" name="__codelineno-0-305"></a><span class="sd">    :param network_idle: Wait for the page until there are no network connections for at least 500 ms.</span>
</span><span id="__span-0-306"><a id="__codelineno-0-306" name="__codelineno-0-306"></a><span class="sd">    :param load_dom: Enabled by default, wait for all JavaScript on page(s) to fully load and execute.</span>
</span><span id="__span-0-307"><a id="__codelineno-0-307" name="__codelineno-0-307"></a><span class="sd">    :param selector_config: The arguments that will be passed in the end while creating the final Selector's class.</span>
</span><span id="__span-0-308"><a id="__codelineno-0-308" name="__codelineno-0-308"></a><span class="sd">    :param proxy: Static proxy to override rotator and session proxy. A new browser context will be created and used with it.</span>
</span><span id="__span-0-309"><a id="__codelineno-0-309" name="__codelineno-0-309"></a><span class="sd">    :return: A `Response` object.</span>
</span><span id="__span-0-310"><a id="__codelineno-0-310" name="__codelineno-0-310"></a><span class="sd">    """</span>
</span><span id="__span-0-311"><a id="__codelineno-0-311" name="__codelineno-0-311"></a>    <span class="n">static_proxy</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">kwargs</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">pop</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="s2">"proxy"</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="kc">None</span><span class="p">)</span>

</span><span id="__span-0-316"><a id="__codelineno-0-316" name="__codelineno-0-316"></a>        <span class="k">raise</span> <span class="ne">RuntimeError</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="s2">"Context manager has been closed"</span><span class="p">)</span>

</span><span id="__span-0-324"><a id="__codelineno-0-324" name="__codelineno-0-324"></a>        <span class="n">proxy</span><span class="p">:</span> <span class="n">Optional</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="n">ProxyType</span><span class="p">]</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="kc">None</span>
</span><span id="__span-0-325"><a id="__codelineno-0-325" name="__codelineno-0-325"></a>        <span class="k">if</span> <span class="bp">self</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">_config</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">proxy_rotator</span> <span class="ow">and</span> <span class="n">static_proxy</span> <span class="ow">is</span> <span class="kc">None</span><span class="p">:</span>
</span><span id="__span-0-326"><a id="__codelineno-0-326" name="__codelineno-0-326"></a>            <span class="n">proxy</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="bp">self</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">_config</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">proxy_rotator</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">get_proxy</span><span class="p">()</span>
